dailyJudgementSnippet: OpenAI won mostly by avoiding a clearly bad HN cycle: the main ChatGPT story had a privacy-hostile framing, but top comments largely reframed the checks as routine anti-abuse work needed to keep free logged-out access available, so the day landed mixed rather than strongly negative [E1].
winnerExplanation: OpenAI finished highest because its only meaningful front-page signal was a mixed discussion about ChatGPT bot-detection, where criticism of Cloudflare-style friction was counterbalanced by multiple comments treating the behavior as understandable abuse prevention for a free product [E1]. That left OpenAI with a steadier day than the field, while no other tracked provider matched it on combined sentiment and coverage.

openai.judgementSnippet: A harshly worded ChatGPT headline raised privacy and usability concerns, but the top discussion repeatedly framed the behavior as expected bot-abuse prevention that helps preserve free access, which kept the overall reaction mixed [E1].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Claude Code took the day's clearest hit from a front-page bug report about repeatedly running destructive `git reset --hard origin/main`, which drew ridicule and concern about unsafe autonomous actions; a separate coding-agents thread only added a small counterweight by describing Claude Code as useful in practice [E2][E3].

evidence.summary: "ChatGPT won't let you type until Cloudflare reads your React state" framed OpenAI as invasive and brittle, but top comments mostly explained it as bot-abuse prevention to protect free logged-out ChatGPT access rather than a uniquely alarming product failure.

"Claude Code runs Git reset –hard origin/main against project repo every 10 mins" generated strong negative reaction, with commenters calling the behavior destructive, absurd, and a sign of unsafe AI automation in development workflows.

In "Coding agents could make free software matter again," a top comment said "Claude Code would be far less useful without grep, diff, git..." and treated it as a genuinely helpful developer tool, providing a modest positive signal for Anthropic.
